items.summary: FSB-linked Gamaredon concealed a fileless worm in NTFS data streams to spy on Ukraine targets

items.title: Microsoft fixes KB5089549 Windows security update install issues
items.summary: Microsoft has resolved a known issue causing installation failures and 0x800f0922 errors when deploying the May 2026 Windows 11 security update (KB5089549). [...]

items.summary: In this excerpt from WIRED Book Club pick The Yahoo Boys, journalist Carlos Barragán traces one scammer’s journey from flop to fortune.

items.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks
items.summary:
Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure.
The post Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI.

The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over

items.title: Websites Can Now Spy on You Through Your Hard Drive
items.summary: Thanks to the newly detailed FROST technique, telltale SSD activity can be measured in the browser using simple JavaScript.

items.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
items.summary:
Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites.

WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps

items.summary: Hackers are targeting WordPress websites running a vulnerable version of the WP Maps Pro plugin, which allows creating rogue administrator accounts without authentication. [...]

items.summary:
Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks.

items.title:
CVE-2024-36137 A vulnerability has been identified in Node.js, affecting users of the experimental permission model when the --allow-fs-write flag is used.

Node.js Permission Model do not operate on file descriptors, however, operations such as fs.fchown or fs.fchmod can use a "read-only" file descriptor to change the owner and permissions of a file.
items.summary: Information published.

items.summary: Palo Alto Networks is warning that hackers are now exploiting a PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, in attacks attempting to breach corporate networks. [...]

items.title: Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say
items.summary:
Moscow’s agents are building fake companies, recruiting middlemen and deploying cyber spies and hackers who gather information that could be used to attack key infrastructure.
The post Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say appeared first on Securi

items.title: Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability
items.summary:
The one-click vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on self-hosted Flowise servers by tricking users into importing a malicious chatflow.
The post Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New CIFSwitch Linux flaw gives root on multiple distributions
items.summary: A newly discovered local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed 'CIFSwitch' in the Linux kernel could allow attackers to forge CIFS authentication key descriptions, abuse the kernel's key request mechanism, and gain root privileges. [...]

items.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
items.summary:
Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), refers to a case of authentication bypass that could be exploited b

items.title: The White House’s Aliens.gov Site Brags That ICE Arrested More Than 700 US Citizens
items.summary: The website, which compares human beings to extraterrestrials, touts arrest numbers from the Trump administration’s sweeping immigration crackdown. But some of its details are really out there.

items.title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware
items.summary: Threat actors are abusing ChatGPT's content-sharing feature to display fake OpenAI outage pages that direct users to download malware disguised as the ChatGPT desktop application. [...]

items.title: California AG sues 23andMe over 2023 breach exposing health data
items.summary: California Attorney General Rob Bonta filed a lawsuit against 23andMe, now Chrome Holding Co., over the company's failure to protect sensitive customer genetic and personal information. [...]

items.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT that leverages the artificial intelligence (AI) assistant's implicit trust in Markdown links and images to trigger prompt injections and open the door to phishing attacks.

The technique has been codenamed ChatGPhi

items.title: Charter Communications Data Breach Could Impact Nearly 5 Million
items.summary:
The notorious ShinyHunters extortion group leaked over 42 million records allegedly stolen from Charter in April.
The post Charter Communications Data Breach Could Impact Nearly 5 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
items.summary:
An unknown threat actor has been observed using a large language model (LLM) agent to conduct post-compromise actions after obtaining initial access following the exploitation of a publicly-accessible Marimo network using a recently disclosed vulnerability.

items.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly in Cybersecurity – Week 22
items.summary: Authorities dismantle Russian-aligned hosting firm, FBI warns of in-person data thefts, and TrapDoor steals credentials via software supply chain attack.

items.title: MokN Raises $15 Million for Phish-Back Platform
items.summary:
MokN's platform deploys realistic decoy access points to lure attackers into revealing compromised credentials, enabling organizations to respond before abuse occurs.
The post MokN Raises $15 Million for Phish-Back Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: From $5 Attacks to Botnet-Powered Platforms: Inside the DDoS-as-a- Service Market
items.summary: DDoS attacks are increasingly being sold like subscription services, complete with pricing tiers, support, and reseller programs. Flare explores how the DDoS-as-a-Service market has evolved from scattered tools into polished attack platforms. [...]

items.title: Dutch govt disrupts malware botnet with 17 million infected devices
items.summary: Dutch authorities have taken offline a massive botnet of 17 million devices and seized more than 200 servers at a local provider that supported the operation. [...]

items.title: Microsoft calls zero-day releases ‘never justifiable’ as researcher threatens to drop more
items.summary: Each vulnerability was published with working proof-of-concept code to the Microsoft-owned code repository GitHub, making them immediately available to both attackers and security professionals.

items.title: Silent Ransom Group Uses In-Person IT Impersonation to Breach Systems
items.summary: Threat actors from the Silent Ransom Group, aka Luna Moth, are escalating attacks by impersonating IT staff in phone calls and even showing up in person to gain direct access to victim systems

items.title: Gogs Zero-Day Exposes Servers to Remote Code Execution
items.summary:
The critical-severity issue, assigned a CVSS score of 9.4, is an argument injection flaw that can be exploited by authenticated attackers via pull requests with malicious branch names.
The post Gogs Zero-Day Exposes Servers to Remote Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Russia-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
items.summary:
A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025.

GREYVIBE, per WithSecure, is assessed to be a Russian-speaking group operating broadly in the Russian time zone, wi

items.title: California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach
items.summary:
Attorney General Rob Bonta filed the lawsuit against Chrome Holding Co., which 23andMe rebranded under after filing for bankruptcy last March.
The post California Sues 23andMe, Alleging It Failed to Protect User Data in 2023 Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.summary:
Shadow AI used to mean employees pasting things they shouldn't into ChatGPT. It now means something bigger: employees building full applications with AI, wiring them into production systems, and publishing them on the open internet. Without Security or IT in the loop.
